title: Executive Brief
subtitle: Sutton SignWriting as research infrastructure
summary: First stewardship anchor for the Sutton SignWriting Infrastructure and Program series.
synopsis: This entry gives the shortest forwardable stewardship case for Sutton SignWriting. It frames the work as an existing global system with real assets, an unmet continuity layer, a stronger empirical base in Brazil, a current institutional proof point in South Korea, and a clear opportunity for a PI or host structure to convert informal stewardship into a durable program.

slides.title: Executive Frame
slides.summary: Sutton SignWriting is existing research infrastructure with real assets and a continuity layer that needs stronger structure.

image_prompts.concrete: Executive brief slide showing existing SignWriting assets, users, standards, software, data, and a stewardship structure around them
image_prompts.abstract: An existing infrastructure base surrounded by a stronger continuity frame

slides.speech: The executive frame is simple. Sutton SignWriting is not a speculative proposal. It is existing research and language infrastructure with real assets, real users, and a continuity layer that needs stronger structure.

slides.title: Asset Base And Gap
slides.summary: The asset base is substantial; the gap is stable stewardship for work that already exists.

image_prompts.concrete: Two-column executive slide with asset base on one side and continuity gap on the other
image_prompts.abstract: A strong asset base next to a clearly named continuity gap

bullets: Brazil and South Korea evidence anchors
slides.speech: The asset base includes the writing system, public texts, software, fonts, data, and standards-facing work. The gap is not absence of value. The gap is stable stewardship for assets that already exist and are already maintained informally.

slides.title: First-Stage Stewardship
slides.summary: A first-stage stewardship structure would fund maintenance, documentation, translation, coordination, and research readiness.

image_prompts.concrete: Stewardship funding slide showing maintenance, documentation, translation, coordination, research readiness, and succession planning
image_prompts.abstract: Named stewardship functions arranged as a practical first stage

slides.speech: First-stage stewardship does not need to solve everything at once. It would fund practical functions: maintenance, documentation, onboarding, translation, partner coordination, and research readiness.
slides.notes: Use functions rather than institutional wish language.

slides.title: What A Host Enables
slides.summary: A PI, host structure, consortium, or fiscal sponsor could convert informal stewardship into an accountable program.

image_prompts.concrete: Institutional pathway slide showing informal stewardship becoming an accountable program through PI, host, consortium, or fiscal sponsor
image_prompts.abstract: Informal maintenance becoming accountable program structure

bullets: A durable base for research and public use
slides.speech: A PI, host structure, consortium, or fiscal sponsor would not create the value from scratch. It would make existing value easier to preserve, fund, coordinate, and extend responsibly.
slides.notes: Close with accountable structure, not rescue language.
